titleEn: UK Firm Takes Leading Role in Cutting-Edge Farm Robots Project
titleZh: 英国公司领衔前沿农场机器人项目，蜜蜂无人机与机器鼹鼠将上岗
summaryZh: 英国工程公司Forth UK成为FARMAR全球项目的行业合作伙伴，该项目旨在将传统农耕知识转化为智能机器人系统。项目将开发蜜蜂无人机和机器鼹鼠等地面与空中机器人，通过AI学习农民日常操作，实现自动化田间巡检与作业，应对劳动力短缺、气候冲击和成本上升等挑战。项目为期四年，涉及多国合作，强调文化传承与技术创新结合。
translationZh:
来自一家领先工程公司的创新者已被任命为一个全球项目的行业合作伙伴，该项目可能将蜜蜂无人机和机器鼹鼠部署到正在运营的农场。

总部位于坎布里亚郡的Forth UK是FARMAR项目的重要组成部分——这是一个创新的全球项目，旨在将传统农耕知识转化为支持现代农业的智能机器人系统。

FARMAR的全称是“农民参与、传承感知的人工智能与机器人机械化促进农业韧性”。这项为期四年的国际合作将于10月启动。

Forth首席执行官马克·特尔福德表示：“我们的重点是为西坎布里亚的中小企业解锁新机遇，依托在塞拉菲尔德锻造的卓越传统。正是这种经验使坎布里亚的企业成为应对全球挑战的最强竞争者之一。”

**超越颠覆：通过种植者主导的创新在农业科技中赢得信任并推动影响**

“Forth完全有条件参与FARMAR。我们的团队能够被选为这样一个前沿项目的行业合作伙伴，是对我们技能的肯定。能够参与如此具有全球意义的事业，真是太棒了。”

“FARMAR可能改变全球农业的运作方式，提高可持续性和效率。这与Forth解决问题的方法一致：创新、测试、创造。”

FARMAR将结合人工智能、机器人技术、作物科学和农民专业知识，支持向更可持续、更有韧性和更高效的农业系统转型。能够直接从真实农业实践中学习的智能地面和空中机器人将在真实的农场环境中运行。

Forth将制作该技术的原型，并与英国、欧洲、亚洲和北美的合作伙伴合作。在每个站点，农民将展示并解释他们的日常工作流程。简短的演示和口头叙述笔记将被转化为机器人可以遵循的分步指令。

一台小型现场计算机将协调轻型地面车辆和无人机团队，这些车辆和无人机负责侦察田地、标记问题并在严格的安全限制和人工监督下执行任务。一个简单的应用程序将允许农民审查和调整机器人的工作。

技术、农业、经济学和社会科学领域的专家将专注于有利于农民的创新，同时尊重数据、法律和文化方面的考量。

FARMAR旨在通过将日常经验知识——以及当地关于如何走田、除草和照料作物的传承——转化为实用且安全的自动化，帮助农场应对现代农业中的挑战，包括劳动力短缺、气候冲击和成本上升。通过将文化背景嵌入现代技术，该项目旨在增强小型农业系统的生产力、可持续性和社区认同感。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了将传统农耕知识与AI、机器人深度融合的前沿实践，蜜蜂无人机和机器鼹鼠等创新概念对智慧畜牧行业具有启发性，尤其适用于需要精细巡检和自动化作业的养殖与种植场景。
contentFull: By Forth Share Robotic bees will be constructed as part of the FARMAR project. This image has been generated using AI. Innovators from a leading engineering company have been appointed industry partner to a global project that could see bee drones and robotic moles dispatched to working farms. Cumbria-based Forth UK is a vital part of FARMAR – an innovative global project translating traditional farming knowledge into intelligent robotic systems to support modern agriculture. FARMAR’s full title is ‘Farmer-in-the-loop heritage-aware AI and Robotic Mechanisation for Agricultural Resilience’. The four-year international collaboration starts in October. Forth CEO Mark Telford said: “Our focus is on unlocking new opportunities for West Cumbrian SMEs, drawing on the pedigree forged at Sellafield. It’s this experience that makes Cumbria’s businesses some of the strongest contenders to meet global challenges head on. Top Articles Read More Read More Read More Read More Read More Beyond Disruption: Earning Trust and Driving Impact in AgTech Through Grower-Led Innovation “Forth is perfectly placed to be involved with FARMAR. It’s a tribute to the skills of our team that we’ve been chosen as an industry partner for such a cutting-edge project. And it’s great to be involved in something of such global significance. “FARMAR could change the way farming works around the world, improving sustainability and efficiency. It aligns with Forth’s approach to problem-solving: innovate, test, create.” FARMAR will combine artificial intelligence, robotics, crop science and farmer expertise to support the transition towards more sustainable, resilient and efficient agricultural systems. Smart ground and aerial robots that learn directly from real farming practices will operate in live farm environments. Forth will make prototypes of the technology, working with partners across the UK, Europe, Asia and North America. On each site, farmers will show and explain their routines. Short demonstrations and spoken narrative notes will be turned into step-by-step instructions that robots can follow. A small on-site computer will coordinate teams of lightweight ground vehicles and drones that scout fields, flag issues and carry out tasks under strict safety limits and with human oversight. A simple app will allow farmers to review and adjust the robots’ work. Experts in technology, agriculture, economics and social sciences will focus on innovations that benefit farmers, while respecting data, legal and cultural considerations. FARMAR aims to help farms respond to challenges in modern agriculture, including labour shortages, climate shocks and rising costs, by turning everyday know-how – and the local heritage of how fields are walked, weeds are managed and crops are cared for – into practical and safe automation. By embedding cultural context into modern technologies, the project seeks to strengthen productivity, sustainability and community identity within small farming systems. Trials will compare FARMAR-assisted work with current practice to fully evaluate the outcomes. Forth is a specialist engineering company delivering smart, practical solutions for the world’s most challenging and safety-critical environments. For more than 25 years it has been developing innovative systems for highly regulated industries, combining technical expertise with hands-on problem-solving. It has a track record in creating, developing, and manufacturing bespoke, purpose-built solutions, to help remove the need for humans to work in hazardous environments. Forth’s pioneering technologies have accelerated nuclear decommissioning projects, enhancing operational safety and significantly reducing costs. Forth also provides innovative and reliable engineering for sectors including the oil and gas industry, renewable energy and water infrastructure, with advanced robotic inspection and maintenance technologies. The FARMAR project has been awarded €1.79 million (about £1.5 million) through the international Horizon Europe programme, which includes UK Government funding. The project will be coordinated by Durham University’s Department of Computer Science. 0 1 5 UK Firm Takes Leading Role in Cutting-Edge Farm Robots Project Subscribe Today For Subscribe Forth is an award-winning UK advanced technology solutions business with bases at Maryport, Cleator Moor and Barrow-in-Furness in Cumbria. author: Matt Hopkins
contentHtml:
Cumbria-based Forth UK is a vital part of FARMAR – an innovative global project translating traditional farming knowledge into intelligent robotic systems to support modern agriculture.
